steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The passage states that disruptions to an enjoyable experience increase enjoyment and that researchers anticipate the opposite effect for an unpleasant experience.

The decisive clue is the phrase ‘opposite effect’, which signals the relationship between the two scenarios.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice B correctly reflects the inverse of the original pattern—where disruptions enhanced enjoyment, they now worsen a negative experience—by describing a negative rating for interrupted listeners compared to uninterrupted ones.

The other three options either reverse the wrong comparison (A), describe a time perception unrelated to emotional response (C), or address an unmentioned sensory change (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
